feature: Three New Features: Creator Profiles, Content Watermarking, Subscription Tiers
summary: All 22 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend tests passed. Three new features are fully functional: (1) Creator Profiles - MongoDB-based profile system with browse, search, and public profile pages, (2) Content Watermarking - customizable text watermarks with preview and settings persistence, (3) Subscription Tiers - 3-tier Stripe subscription with checkout integration.

backend_api_tests.test: POST /api/creator-profiles - creates new profile or returns 400 if exists
GET /api/creator-profiles/browse - returns public profiles list (no auth required)
GET /api/creator-profiles/u/bigmann - returns public profile by username
GET /api/watermark/settings - returns default or saved settings
POST /api/watermark/preview - accepts image and returns watermarked image
GET /api/subscriptions/me - returns current subscription (defaults to free)
POST /api/subscriptions/checkout - creates Stripe checkout session for pro tier
All endpoints accessible and properly registered

frontend_tests.details: Login works, Creator Profile and Plans links visible in nav
Shows profile card with display name 'John LeGerron Spivey', username '@bigmann', genres (Hip Hop, R&B, Pop), stats (Assets, Streams, Followers)
My Profile and Browse Creators tabs work
Settings panel with text input, position dropdown, opacity/font-size/rotation sliders, color picker, upload area, Save Settings and Preview buttons
3 tier cards: Free ($0), Pro ($9.99/mo), Enterprise ($29.99/mo) with features and upgrade buttons

test_credentials.note: Owner has existing profile with username 'bigmann'. Use cveadmin for testing profile creation.

context_for_next_testing_agent: Three new features are fully working: Creator Profiles (MongoDB-based), Content Watermarking (PIL-based with settings persistence), Subscription Tiers (Stripe checkout integration). All routes registered in App.js (lines 225-227). Navigation links in NavigationBar.jsx. Owner account has profile with username 'bigmann'. Stripe checkout creates real session URLs. Profile browse endpoint is public, others require auth. PostgreSQL RDS timeout errors in logs are expected and unrelated to these MongoDB-based features.
